channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Republicans Begin to Test the Limits of Trump’s Power by Flexing Their Own
item.description: The president’s unilateral and retributive style of governing is starting to hit a wall in both chambers of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rogers
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: President Trump departing the White House in May. On Wednesday, four House Republicans sided with Democrats to demand Mr. Trump withdraw U.S. forces from the conflict with Iran.

item.title: House Passes Iran War Powers Resolution in Bipartisan Rebuke to Trump
item.description: A measure to direct an end to U.S. engagement in Iran was adopted with a handful of Republicans in support, sending a signal of opposition to the president’s handling of the war.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison and Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The vote on Wednesday was the latest reflection of divisions between Republicans in Congress and President Trump on a range of issues as their interests diverge in the run-up to the midterm congressional elections.

item.title: Massie, 3 Other House Republicans Broke From Trump on Iran War Powers Vote
item.description: Four Republicans from different ideological factions crossed party lines to vote with Democrats in favor of reining in the president’s power to wage war unilaterally.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: G.O.P. Defectors Break With Trump Over War, and SpaceX Set for Largest I.P.O. Ever
item.description: Plus, could streetlights be making your allergies worse?
item.dc:creator: Tracy Mumford, Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart, Robert Jimison and Harry Stevens
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Capitol Hill in May. The vote on the war in Iran on Wednesday was the latest reflection of divisions between Republicans in Congress and President Trump on a range of issues.

item.title: Hezbollah’s Fiber-Optic Drones Expose Cracks in Israeli Defenses
item.description: The Lebanese militant group’s attacks have caught Israelis off guard, forcing its political and military leaders to scramble for solutions.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon and Natan Odenheimer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Israeli soldiers reacting to drones near the Israel-Lebanon border on Monday.

item.title: Why Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass Has to Fight for Her Job
item.description: Ms. Bass, a Democrat, has had a roller-coaster first term. She now faces the first runoff since 2005 for an incumbent mayor in the city.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan
item.media:description: Mayor Karen Bass, center, campaigning at a community center in Plaza de Valle in Los Angeles, last month.

item.title: The Democrats’ Big Decision: Black Representation or More Blue Seats?
item.description: As Republicans break up majority-Black House districts, Democrats must decide whether to preserve seats concentrated in urban areas or push them into white suburbs to target G.O.P. seats.
item.dc:creator: Clyde McGrady
item.media:credit: Will Crooks for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative James Clyburn addressing the crowd at his annual fish fry alongside Representative Ro Khanna of California, second from left, and Governor Andy Beshear of Kentucky in Columbia, S.C., on Friday.

item.title: ‘Teachers Are Going to Hate It’: How Social Media Apps Hooked Teens at School
item.description: Internal documents show how tech giants grabbed children’s attention throughout the day, a strategy that schools say has undermined education.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Valentino-DeVries
item.media:credit: Yana Paskova for The New York Times
item.media:description: Social media companies’ push to reach students has often overshadowed concerns from parents and teachers.

item.title: Nathan Lane and Laurie Metcalf on Their Tony-Nominated ‘Death of a Salesman’
item.description: The stars Nathan Lane and Laurie Metcalf talk with the show’s director, Joe Mantello, about the exhilaration of collaborating and the trap of sentimentality.
item.dc:creator: Erik Piepenburg
item.media:credit: Ye Fan for The New York Times
item.media:description: From left, Nathan Lane, Joe Mantello and Laurie Metcalf in Lane’s dressing room at the Winter Garden Theater in Manhattan after a recent performance of “Death of a Salesman.”

item.title: SpaceX IPO to Be Largest Ever at $135 Share Price
item.description: The $135 share price means Elon Musk’s rocket maker is poised to exceed the 2019 initial public offering of Saudi Aramco in both valuation and money raised.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:description: SpaceX’s Starship rocket lifted off last month during a test flight from Starbase in Texas.

item.title: Inside SpaceX’s Rockets-to-Riches I.P.O. Plan
item.description: The latest prospectus by Elon Musk’s company underscores its confidence in its planned market debut. But skeptics see reasons for concern.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe and Lauren Hirsch
item.media:description: SpaceX appears confident that its expected I.P.O. will soar.

item.title: Marjane Satrapi, the Author of ‘Persepolis,’ Dies at 56
item.description: Her popular graphic novel series, published in the early 2000s, followed an Iranian girl through the Islamic Revolution and the Iran-Iraq War.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Ségolène Le Stradic
item.media:description: Marjane Satrapi in 2023. She was one of the best-known exponents of a form of graphic novel that combined political history and memoir.

item.title: The U.S.-Qatar Domination of Gas Left the World Dangerously Exposed
item.description: Before the war, the global market for liquefied natural gas was increasingly commanded by just two countries, one of which has now been hobbled.
item.dc:creator: River Akira Davis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: The liquefied natural gas production facility in Qatar’s Ras Laffan Industrial City before the war. Iranian attacks have crippled its production, and the damage will take years to repair.

item.title: Sherrill Says Immigration Officials Won’t Let Her Visit Detention Center
item.description: The governor said the Department of Homeland Security’s actions at Delaney Hall raise “serious questions.”
item.dc:creator: Tracey Tully
item.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
item.media:description: Gov. Mikie Sherrill of New Jersey and Senator Andy Kim, right, have expressed concerns about conditions at the Delaney Hall immigration detention center.

item.title: E.U. Steps Up Ocean Monitoring as Trump Administration Backs Away
item.description: Days after the U.S. said it would kill a network of ocean monitors, European officials pledged to invest more in their version, calling it a “necessity.”
item.dc:creator: Chico Harlan
item.media:description: The European plan to invest roughly $107 million comes as the U.S. has said it would dismantle a sprawling network of sensors.

item.title: Priest’s Ebola Death Stirs Fear and Doubt in Congolese Town
item.description: Priest’s Ebola Death Stirs Fear an Doubt in Congolese Town
item.dc:creator: Arlette Bashizi
item.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times

item.title: Amid War, Iran’s Soccer Leader Works to Get His Team to the World Cup
item.description: Mehdi Taj said his federation has been dealing with FIFA, not the United States, in trying to obtain visas for Iran to play in the tournament that begins next week.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Mehdi Taj being interviewed by a reporter in Tehran last month. Mr. Taj said mixed messages from Washington had created confusion over Iran’s participation in the World Cup.

item.title: Colombia’s Yellow World Cup Jersey, Once a Symbol of Unity, Becomes a Political Statement
item.description: A presidential candidate endorsed by President Trump has been accused by some Colombians of co-opting their beloved national team’s yellow soccer jersey.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní and Annie Correal
item.media:credit: Federico Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: Supporters of Abelardo De La Espriella wearing national soccer jerseys at a rally in Medellín on May 24.

item.title: The Small-Business Owners Managing Whole Armies of A.I. Employees
item.description: When you turn A.I. agents loose on your finances, email and customers, what could possibly go wrong?
item.dc:creator: Clive Thompson

item.title: Should You Outsource Your Morning Routine to a Chatbot?
item.description: A.I. wants to help you start the day. Its suggestions? Drink coffee and get dressed.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Haigney

item.title: Songwriter David Baerwald Shares an Unspeakable Family Secret in ‘The Fire Agent’
item.description: In “The Fire Agent,” the musician David Baerwald bases his debut novel on the shadowy life of his German-Jewish grandfather: an aristocrat, a soldier, a Buddhist and a spy.
item.dc:creator: Celia McGee and Landon Speers
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Book Review: ‘The Fire Agent,’ by David Baerwald
item.description: “The Fire Agent,” by David Baerwald, is a historical novel that spans two continents and world wars.
item.dc:creator: Dan Fesperman

item.title: With His New Museum, Obama Offers a Trip to a Parallel America
item.description: The presidential center that opens this month seems out of step with the zeitgeist of the moment and produces emotional reactions among the discontented of the Trump era.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker and Jamie Kelter Davis

item.title: Melinda French Gates: Women, We Deserve Better Than This
item.description: Too many women walk out of their doctors’ office with no diagnosis, no treatment and no plan.
item.dc:creator: Melinda French Gates

item.title: Grow, Hair, Grow
item.description: You spend your whole childhood trying to look different from your dad, only to wake up and see his forehead in the mirror.
item.dc:creator: Sameh Alaa

item.title: Look Closely
item.description: Seeing protests through a camera lens.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times

item.title: Late Night Reacts to Another Reality Star Wooing Voters
item.description: Michael Kosta wasn’t impressed to see that the L.A. mayoral candidate Spencer Pratt “got a second-season pickup” after Tuesday’s midterms.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Ireland, Seen as a Weak Link in Europe’s Defense, Is Trying to Bulk Up
item.description: As concern rises in Europe over threats from an emboldened Russia, the Irish government says it’s working to plug gaps in its military, which reflect a tradition of neutrality.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia and Paulo Nunes Dos Santos
